items.title: MidDay Snapshot: AECO Natural Gas Climbs as LNG Canada Demand Spikes
items.summary: Spot natural gas trading was mixed on Monday, with steep Northeast losses skewing the overall average downward on a rapidly warming weather outlook.

items.title: What to Know About Iran: How War in the Middle East is Roiling Natural Gas Markets
items.summary: A disruption in vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz threatens to delay 21–25% of monthly global LNG supply.

items.title: U.S. Gas in Storage Headed Toward Normal Levels Exiting Winter
items.summary: NGI is forecasting the latest U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) Weekly Natural Gas Storage Report through the week ended Feb. 27 to show a withdrawal of 120 Bcf.

items.title: Prolonged War in Middle East Could Send Natural Gas Prices Soaring Higher in Weeks Ahead
items.summary: Global energy prices moved sharply higher on Monday and have the potential to skyrocket as there appears to be no end in sight to the war that broke out across the Middle East over the weekend.

items.title: MidDay Futures: Iran War Impacting Natural Gas as LNG Supply Threatened
items.summary: Escalating conflict in the Middle East interrupted production and maritime transport of LNG, with global price impacts rippling through the U.S. market as of midday Monday.

items.title: Spot LNG Shipping Rates Surge, U.S. Arb Grows on Persian Gulf Security Fears
items.summary: Daily spot rates for shipping LNG to the Atlantic and Pacific shot up by almost half Monday with higher rates seen ahead in the coming days as war in the Middle East shuffles global vessel traffic.

items.title: Midmorning Markets: April Natural Gas Contract Falls Back From $3 as Technicals Cap Rally
items.summary: Natural gas futures retreated from early gains Monday as chart resistance near $3.00/MMBtu stalled a rebound rally, even as escalating military conflict in the Middle East roiled global energy markets.

items.title: Pemex Hits 11-Year Debt Low as 2025 Gas Output Struggles to Stabilize
items.summary: Petróleos Mexicanos (Pemex) closed 2025 on a financial high note by slashing its total debt to an 11-year low of $84 billion, even as the state-owned giant continued to grapple with a volatile production landscape.

items.title: Futures Settle: Lower 48 Natural Gas Prices Choppy as Specter of Wider Middle East War Looms Large
items.summary: Natural gas futures advanced on Monday as traders looked beyond the coming shoulder season and fixated instead on the United States-led attack on Iran and potential fallout that could inject prolonged volatility into energy markets. Gas prices fluctu

items.title: U.S. LNG Arb to Asia Strengthens Amid War as Competition With Europe Heats Up
items.summary: Asian LNG prices were jolted higher Monday as the market weighed the impact of limited natural gas exports from Oman, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates in the coming weeks amid war in the Middle East.

items.title: Pakistan Reroutes Oil Supply as Strait of Hormuz Crisis Escalates
items.summary: Pakistan could seek to be included in Saudi Arabia’s preferred buyers of crude exported from the Red Sea amid the major disruption to tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, sources told Pakistani outlet The News.   The escalating conflict in Iran an

items.title: Chevron Declares Force Majeure as Israel Shuts Leviathan Gas Field
items.summary: Chevron has declared force majeure at Israel’s Leviathan natural gas field after the government ordered a temporary suspension of production on security grounds, marking the second time in less than a year that hostilities with Iran have disrupted Ea

items.title: Iran War Sends Oil Curve Into Crisis Mode
items.summary: What has been happening in the Middle East when it comes to markets won’t affect only headlines but could reshape the structure of the oil market from the physical barrel to the far end of the derivatives curve. As tensions escalate, physical traders

items.title: Arctic Air Drives Natural Gas Volatility Across the Northeast
items.summary: Winter in the Mid-Atlantic region is certainly winding down, and March is always an unpredictable month. Early next week, a fast-moving Alberta Clipper is forecast to bring yet another round of wintry weather to the region before a spring warm-up arr

items.title: JPMorgan: Oil Prices Could Hit $120 Per Barrel
